steps.title: Identify what the blank must mean
steps.content: Wang argues that prediction failures should not lead campaigns to ______ polling entirely. The sentence says polling has value beyond prediction — so the blank describes the tempting but wrong response to those failures: abandoning or ignoring polling altogether.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices, confirm the right one
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'distort': “Distort” means to twist or misrepresent polling — not to stop using it. Prediction failures would lead campaigns to abandon polling, not misrepresent it. ✗
  • Choice B — 'neglect': “Neglect” means to fail to give proper attention to or disregard entirely — exactly the response Wang warns against. Campaigns might be tempted to stop relying on polling after failures, and Wang says they should not. ✓
  • Choice C — 'supplement': “Supplement” means to add more — the opposite direction from what prediction failures would motivate, so Wang would have no reason to argue against it. ✗
  • Choice D — 'enact': “Enact” means to make into law — entirely unrelated to the act of using or ignoring election polling. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
